sources.writer_profile: Food writer at IO AMO Firenze, a publication focused on Florence's culinary scene and local dining culture. Elena Farinelli specializes in restaurant recommendations and guides readers through the city's diverse food landscape.

sources.article_title: Le migliori trattorie di Firenze: tradizione, lampredotto e nuove tavole
sources.writer_profile: Food writer for Gambero Rosso, Italy's leading culinary publication known for its authoritative restaurant guides and wine ratings. Antonella De Santis specializes in exploring traditional Italian dining establishments, with recent coverage focusing on Florence's best trattorias and the evolution of Tuscan culinary traditions.

sources.article_title: Where to eat and stay in Florence 2023
sources.writer_profile: Food writer and cookbook author covering authentic Italian cuisine and travel through her dedicated Substack publication. Emiko Davies specializes in regional Italian cooking and provides insider guides to dining and accommodations in Italy's most beloved cities. Her work combines culinary expertise with practical travel advice for food-focused travelers exploring Italy.

sources.article_title: Florence Food Guide 2026: Best Restaurants, Bars & Street Food (Local's Picks)
sources.writer_profile: Food writer and Florence expert at Girl in Florence, specializing in comprehensive local dining guides for Italy's Renaissance capital. Georgette Jupe creates detailed restaurant recommendations covering everything from fine dining establishments to authentic street food, helping visitors discover the best of Florence's culinary scene through a local's perspective.

sources.article_title: I migliori ristoranti dove mangiare in Oltrarno a Firenze tra San Frediano e Santo Spirito
sources.writer_profile: Food writer for CiboToday, specializing in local restaurant discovery and neighborhood dining guides. Vivian Petrini focuses on uncovering the best culinary experiences in specific areas, including detailed coverage of Florence's Oltrarno district dining scene.

restaurants.name: Trattoria Sergio Gozzi
restaurants.quote: One of the best local options serving traditional Tuscan fare since 1915 — open for lunch-only, sadly depriving the city of its immense talents at all other times.
restaurants.article_title: Florence Food Guide 2026: Best Restaurants, Bars & Street Food (Local's Picks)

restaurants.name: Trattoria Sostanza
restaurants.quote: Una trattoria che vanta 150 anni: venne fondata nel 1869, all'epoca di Firenze Capitale del Regno d'Italia, da Pasquale Campolmi che rilevò una macelleria per metter su la tipica osteria con mescita di vini e pizzicheria.
restaurants.article_title: Mi consigli un ristorante?

restaurants.name: Trattoria Da Burde
restaurants.quote: One of my favourite places for true Tuscan food in Florence — the Gori brothers are well known around town and it is mainly open for lunch.
restaurants.article_title: Where to eat and stay in Florence 2023

restaurants.name: Il Santo Bevitore
restaurants.quote: Negli anni questo ristorante è cresciuto molto, pur mantenendo la sua identità. Si mangia in un ambiente molto romantico, con piatti gustosi, e si beve molto bene.
restaurants.article_title: Mi consigli un ristorante?

restaurants.name: I'Brindellone
restaurants.quote: True no-frills experience with rustic atmosphere and hearty food — fried coccoli, truffle pasta, fried artichokes, ribollita and pappa al pomodoro in a locals' joint in Piazza Piattellina.
restaurants.article_title: Florence Food Guide 2026: Best Restaurants, Bars & Street Food (Local's Picks)

restaurants.name: Gunè
restaurants.quote: Haute cuisine fusing Basilicata and Tuscan traditions with three tasting menus at €55–120, plus a cocktail bar called Next Door for pre- and post-dinner drinks.
restaurants.article_title: I migliori ristoranti dove mangiare in Oltrarno a Firenze tra San Frediano e Santo Spirito

restaurants.name: Cuculia
restaurants.quote: Elegant international restaurant with Venezuelan-French-Spanish influences by chef Oliver Betancourt, serving tasting menus at €90–110 in a refined space at Via dei Serragli 3.
restaurants.article_title: I migliori ristoranti dove mangiare in Oltrarno a Firenze tra San Frediano e Santo Spirito

restaurants.name: Io Osteria Personale
restaurants.quote: Seasonal dishes that depart from traditional Tuscan cuisine to become more contemporary and creative, with tasting menus at €50–66 in a former delicatessen at Borgo San Frediano.
restaurants.article_title: I migliori ristoranti dove mangiare in Oltrarno a Firenze tra San Frediano e Santo Spirito

restaurants.name: Osteria Tripperia Il Magazzino
restaurants.quote: A temple dedicated to organ meats situated between Santo Spirito basilica, Palazzo Pitti, and the Lungarno, with exposed brick vaulted ceilings and uncompromising Tuscan cooking at reasonable prices; signature dishes include beef tongue carpaccio, lampredotto meatballs, and duck pappardelle.
restaurants.article_title: Le migliori trattorie di Firenze: tradizione, lampredotto e nuove tavole

restaurants.name: Trattoria Mario
restaurants.quote: non puoi venire a Firenze senza provare Mario. Esercizio storico dietro al mercato di San Lorenzo, aperto solo a pranzo, negli anni pre-pandemia era inavvicinabile a causa delle lunghe code.
restaurants.article_title: Mi consigli un ristorante?

restaurants.name: Trattoria Sabatino
restaurants.quote: Prezzi così bassi non si trovano facilmente a Firenze. E neppure un menù che ti fa pensare di essere a casa della nonna, fra quarti di pollo, minestrone e verdure condite.
restaurants.article_title: Mi consigli un ristorante?

restaurants.name: Antica Trattoria da Tito
restaurants.quote: Nell'allegro e informale servizio di questa trattoria spicca la simpatia del suo proprietario Bobo. Mentre aspettate la comanda guardatevi intorno, tanti spunti per far sorridere.
restaurants.article_title: Mi consigli un ristorante?

restaurants.name: Il Guscio
restaurants.quote: Anche se più giovane di altri nomi storici (aperta dal 1986) questa ex tripperia si è guadagnata un pubblico di fidelizzati nel quartiere di San Frediano. Propone anche piatti di pesce.
restaurants.article_title: Mi consigli un ristorante?

restaurants.name: Le Volte
restaurants.quote: Se amate la carne questo posto è per voi. Il proprietario è il figlio dei macellai all'angolo. Da provare: oltre alla tagliata, la tartare.
restaurants.article_title: Mi consigli un ristorante?

restaurants.name: Trattoria Napoleone
restaurants.quote: Bello sia in estate, quando si mangia con la vista sulla chiesa, che in inverno, nelle belle sale interne. Da provare: i piatti a base di tartufo.
restaurants.article_title: Mi consigli un ristorante?

restaurants.name: Osteria di Giovanni
restaurants.quote: Giovanni è uno dei figli di Narcisio Latini che nel 2004 ha aperto a poca distanza un ristorante di cucina toscana con qualche innovazione.
restaurants.article_title: Mi consigli un ristorante?

restaurants.name: Boccanegra
restaurants.quote: Un ristorante con più anime: l'osteria toscana, la pizza, la carne, e le sale per gli eventi. Su tutto una grande qualità degli ingredienti e un servizio professionale.
restaurants.article_title: Mi consigli un ristorante?

restaurants.name: La Giostra
restaurants.quote: Nascosto in una strada defilata, questo ristorante dall'atmosfera suggestiva – bellissime le lucine sul soffitto – ha visto passare attori, cantanti e personaggi famosi. Forse perchè è gestito dal Principe Soldano Kunz Asburgo Lorena?
restaurants.article_title: Mi consigli un ristorante?

restaurants.name: Da Ruggero
restaurants.quote: su per la salita di via Senese, che da porta Romana si avvia verso fuori città, questa trattoria è un punto di riferimento per i locali e quei turisti che si avventurano fuori dal centro storico.
restaurants.article_title: Mi consigli un ristorante?

restaurants.name: Irene
restaurants.quote: Seduti fra i tavolini di piazza Repubblica o negli splendidi interni del 5 stelle del gruppo Rocco Forte, nel bistrò Irene potete assaporare i piatti ideati dalla direzione di Fulvio Pierangelini.
restaurants.article_title: Mi consigli un ristorante?

restaurants.name: L'Ortone
dishes_mentioned: fried tongue with caramelized onion and lime yogurt
restaurants.quote: Owner Federico Tacconi oversees a welcoming space at Sant'Ambrogio market square; the kitchen shows territorial attention and contemporary sensibility with seasonal ingredients — including fried tongue with caramelized onion and lime yogurt, and crispy octopus with pea purée and feta foam.
restaurants.article_title: Le migliori trattorie di Firenze: tradizione, lampredotto e nuove tavole

restaurants.name: Cecchini in città
restaurants.quote: Chef Dario Cecchini's first Florence restaurant at the 25hours Hotel near Santa Maria Novella, featuring a central grill and two formats: the fixed Cecchini Griglia (tartare, carpaccio, bistecca fiorentina at €70) and an à la carte Famiglia option with fresh pasta.
restaurants.article_title: Le migliori trattorie di Firenze: tradizione, lampredotto e nuove tavole

restaurants.name: Procacci
restaurants.quote: Go here for a fancy aperitivo of truffled panini and great Virgin Marys.
restaurants.article_title: Where to eat and stay in Florence 2023

restaurants.name: Le Volpi e l'Uva
restaurants.quote: My long-standing favorite since 2005 — the perfect small wine bar in Florence, where owner Ciro expertly guides you through the list.
restaurants.article_title: Where to eat and stay in Florence 2023

restaurants.name: Regina Bistecca
restaurants.quote: Go here for the works or a special night out — with a great bar for pre-dinner drinks in a space that opened inside a former antiquarian bookshop.
restaurants.article_title: Where to eat and stay in Florence 2023

restaurants.name: Alassio
restaurants.quote: Chef Luca Marin, formerly of Il Santo Bevitore, does polished comfort cooking: beef tartare with seed mustard and wild-boar-stuffed ravioli in a relaxed, buzzing space.
restaurants.article_title: Florence Food Guide 2026: Best Restaurants, Bars & Street Food (Local's Picks)

restaurants.name: Semel
dishes_mentioned: sandwiches with anchovies, fennel and orange
restaurants.quote: Quintessential Florentine spot — think cubby holes for tiny wine glasses and small sandwiches with high-quality ingredients like anchovies, fennel and orange eaten standing up.
restaurants.article_title: Florence Food Guide 2026: Best Restaurants, Bars & Street Food (Local's Picks)

restaurants.name: Teatro del Sale
restaurants.quote: Casual atmosphere served buffet style with an abundance of fantastic food — cheap and wonderful, and a true Florence institution beloved by locals.
restaurants.article_title: Florence Food Guide 2026: Best Restaurants, Bars & Street Food (Local's Picks)

restaurants.name: Zeb
restaurants.quote: Refined Tuscan cuisine in a cozy yet contemporary ambiance — seasonal menu, fresh pastas, and great people-watching from the bar at Via San Miniato.
restaurants.article_title: Florence Food Guide 2026: Best Restaurants, Bars & Street Food (Local's Picks)

restaurants.name: Osteria dell'Enoteca
dishes_mentioned: fave bean and pecorino cheese tortelli
restaurants.quote: Focuses on Tuscan food with a little flair — fresh pasta, fave bean and pecorino cheese tortelli, baccalà on polenta, and a special exclusive grilled meats and steak menu.
restaurants.article_title: Florence Food Guide 2026: Best Restaurants, Bars & Street Food (Local's Picks)

restaurants.name: Trattoria La Casalinga
restaurants.quote: True Tuscan institution and one of my favorite places — penne all'arrabiata, fettunta appetizers, ribollita, with pasta starting around 7€.
restaurants.article_title: Florence Food Guide 2026: Best Restaurants, Bars & Street Food (Local's Picks)

restaurants.name: Trattoria Dell'Orto
restaurants.quote: Where you want to go to eat traditional Tuscan food in a rustic ambiance — fantastic steak in the Via dell'Orto.
restaurants.article_title: Florence Food Guide 2026: Best Restaurants, Bars & Street Food (Local's Picks)

restaurants.name: Trattoria Giovanni
restaurants.quote: Menu impressively affordable and changes seasonally — around 11 euros for first course, second course, side dish and water at Via Sant'Agostino.
restaurants.article_title: Florence Food Guide 2026: Best Restaurants, Bars & Street Food (Local's Picks)

restaurants.name: Essenziale
restaurants.quote: Chef Simone Cipriani's project in Piazza di Cestello offers three gastronomic experiences: a 7-course casual fine dining tasting menu at €80, a fast-casual option at €8–18, and thematic communal dinners at €35.
restaurants.article_title: I migliori ristoranti dove mangiare in Oltrarno a Firenze tra San Frediano e Santo Spirito

restaurants.name: L'OV Osteria Vegetariana
restaurants.quote: One of Florence's most reliable addresses for vegetarian cuisine, with creative seasonal dishes like avocado ceviche at Piazza del Carmine.
restaurants.article_title: I migliori ristoranti dove mangiare in Oltrarno a Firenze tra San Frediano e Santo Spirito

restaurants.name: Cestello Firenze
restaurants.quote: Seafood specialist emphasizing raw preparations with a 'Crudo' tasting menu at €130, in a space overlooking the historic San Frediano church at Piazza di Cestello.
restaurants.article_title: I migliori ristoranti dove mangiare in Oltrarno a Firenze tra San Frediano e Santo Spirito
restaurants.geo_matched_name: Chiesa di San Frediano in Cestello

restaurants.name: All'Antico Ristoro di Cambi
restaurants.quote: Historic tavern founded in 1950 in a brick-vaulted dining room, renowned for bistecca alla fiorentina and traditional offal dishes including lampredotto, with excellent pici pasta.
restaurants.article_title: I migliori ristoranti dove mangiare in Oltrarno a Firenze tra San Frediano e Santo Spirito

restaurants.name: 5 e Cinque
restaurants.quote: Simple vegetarian cuisine featuring cecina (chickpea flatbread), Ligurian focaccia di Recco, and organic seasonal homemade pasta in a neighbourhood spot near Borgo San Frediano.
restaurants.article_title: I migliori ristoranti dove mangiare in Oltrarno a Firenze tra San Frediano e Santo Spirito

restaurants.name: Cucina
restaurants.quote: Contemporary bistrot opened in Oltrarno with a menu conceived each morning after shopping, blending Moroccan and Oriental influences on Tuscan tradition with quality seasonal ingredients.
restaurants.article_title: I migliori ristoranti dove mangiare in Oltrarno a Firenze tra San Frediano e Santo Spirito

restaurants.name: Gurdulù
restaurants.quote: Hybrid restaurant-enoteca-deli serving traditional offal dishes, daily vegetable specials, and fresh pasta in a space that doubles as a neighbourhood gastronomia.
restaurants.article_title: I migliori ristoranti dove mangiare in Oltrarno a Firenze tra San Frediano e Santo Spirito

restaurants.name: Dalla Lola
restaurants.quote: Young trattoria run by chef Matilde Pettini highlighting forgotten Tuscan traditions, with evolving menus that bring international spice influences to classic Florentine ingredients.
restaurants.article_title: I migliori ristoranti dove mangiare in Oltrarno a Firenze tra San Frediano e Santo Spirito

restaurants.name: Momoyama
restaurants.quote: Japanese fusion restaurant established in 1998 at Borgo San Frediano, offering sushi, sashimi, and cooked dishes including gyoza and tempura with an extensive sake and wine selection.
restaurants.article_title: I migliori ristoranti dove mangiare in Oltrarno a Firenze tra San Frediano e Santo Spirito
